critical_severity_vulnerabilities.title: CVE-2020-2551 (Oracle WebLogic Server Deserialization Vulnerability)
critical_severity_vulnerabilities.description: Critical deserialization vulnerability affecting Oracle WebLogic Server.
critical_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest security patches from Oracle for WebLogic Server to address CVE-2020-2551. Consider restricting access to the /console/login/LoginForm.jsp path if it's not strictly necessary for public access.

high_severity_vulnerabilities.title: SSL POODLE information leak (CVE-2014-3566)
high_severity_vulnerabilities.description: The SSL protocol 3.0, as used in OpenSSL through 1.0.1i and other products, uses nondeterministic CBC padding, which makes it easier for man-in-the-middle attackers to obtain cleartext data via a padding-oracle attack, aka the "POODLE" issue.
high_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Disable SSL 3.0 support on affected servers and ensure only TLS 1.2 or higher is used. Configure web servers and clients to prioritize strong ciphers and avoid CBC padding in SSL/TLS.

raw.@output:
VULNERABLE:
  SSL POODLE information leak
    State: LIKELY VULNERABLE
    IDs:  BID:70574  CVE:CVE-2014-3566
          The SSL protocol 3.0, as used in OpenSSL through 1.0.1i and other
          products, uses nondeterministic CBC padding, which makes it easier
          for man-in-the-middle attackers to obtain cleartext data via a
          padding-oracle attack, aka the "POODLE" issue.
    Disclosure date: 2014-10-14
    Check results:
      TLS_DHE_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA
      TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV properly implemented
    References:

      https://cve.mitre.org/cgi-bin/cvename.cgi?name=CVE-2014-3566

high_severity_vulnerabilities.title: CVE-2022-21371 (Oracle WebLogic Server Vulnerability)
high_severity_vulnerabilities.description: High severity vulnerability affecting Oracle WebLogic Server, potentially leading to information disclosure or remote code execution.
high_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest security patches from Oracle for WebLogic Server to address CVE-2022-21371. Review and restrict access to sensitive paths like './/WEB-INF/weblogic.xml'.

high_severity_vulnerabilities.title: CVE-2023-22047 (Oracle WebLogic Server XML External Entity (XXE) Vulnerability)
high_severity_vulnerabilities.description: High severity XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability in Oracle WebLogic Server, potentially allowing for local file inclusion.
high_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the latest security patches from Oracle for WebLogic Server to address CVE-2023-22047. Configure WebLogic Server to disable DTD processing or external entity resolution if not required.

medium_severity_vulnerabilities.title: Apache byterange filter DoS (CVE-2011-3192)
medium_severity_vulnerabilities.description: The Apache web server is vulnerable to a denial of service attack when numerous overlapping byte ranges are requested.
medium_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade Apache HTTP Server to a version that patches CVE-2011-3192 (e.g., 2.2.21 or later). Implement rate limiting or WAF rules to detect and block requests with excessive or overlapping byte ranges.

medium_severity_vulnerabilities.title: behat-config (Exposed Behat Configuration File)
medium_severity_vulnerabilities.description: A Behat configuration file (.yml.dist) is exposed, which could contain sensitive information about application testing and structure.
medium_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove or restrict access to the 'behat.yml.dist' file. Ensure that development or configuration files are never publicly accessible in a production environment.

low_severity_vulnerabilities.title: Firewall Bypass through FTP helper (IPv4)
low_severity_vulnerabilities.description: The firewall is vulnerable to bypass through the FTP helper, potentially allowing unintended traffic through.
low_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Review firewall rules and configurations related to FTP helper modules. Ensure that FTP helper is correctly configured or disabled if not needed, and that it adheres to the principle of least privilege.

raw.@output: Firewall vulnerable to bypass through ftp helper. (IPv4)

low_severity_vulnerabilities.title: JMX-console authentication not required (CVE-2010-0738)
low_severity_vulnerabilities.description: The JMX console is accessible without authentication, potentially exposing sensitive management interfaces.
low_severity_vulnerabilities.remediation: Secure the JMX console by requiring authentication. Restrict access to the JMX console from untrusted networks. Review and implement strong access controls for all management interfaces.

immediate: Address the Critical CVE-2020-2551 vulnerability on sis.nmu.edu.eg by applying the latest Oracle WebLogic Server security patches.
Disable SSL 3.0 on sis.nmu.edu.eg and ensure TLS 1.2 or higher is enforced to mitigate the POODLE vulnerability (CVE-2014-3566).
Review and patch Oracle WebLogic Server instances on sis.nmu.edu.eg for CVE-2022-21371 and CVE-2023-22047.
Remove or secure the exposed 'behat.yml.dist' file on nlms.nmu.edu.eg immediately.
Upgrade Apache HTTP Server on ep.nmu.edu.eg and qb.nmu.edu.eg to a version patched against CVE-2011-3192.
short_term: Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to provide an additional layer of protection against known web vulnerabilities for all public-facing web services.
Conduct a comprehensive review of all public-facing services (HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, MySQL) to ensure only necessary ports are open and services are configured securely.
Enforce strong authentication and authorization mechanisms for all administrative interfaces, including the JMX console found on qb.nmu.edu.eg.
Perform regular vulnerability scanning and penetration testing on all exposed assets.
Establish a robust patch management program to ensure all systems and applications are kept up-to-date with the latest security fixes.
medium_term: Develop and implement a security baseline configuration for all web servers (Nginx, Apache, IIS) and application servers (WebLogic).
Initiate a security awareness training program for development and operations teams focusing on secure coding practices and common vulnerabilities.
Evaluate and implement a centralized logging and monitoring solution to detect and respond to security incidents more effectively.
Consider segmenting the network to limit the blast radius of potential compromises.
Regularly review and update incident response plans based on identified vulnerabilities and threats.
